label: RSI (14)
definition: Momentum oscillator from 0–100; how hot or cold the recent move is.
how_computed: Wilder's RSI over the last 14 daily closes.
good_when: 30–70 is neutral; <30 oversold (possible add timing); >70 overbought (don't chase).

label: MACD
definition: Trend/momentum crossover signal.
how_computed: EMA(12) − EMA(26) vs its 9-day signal line; we read the latest crossover.
good_when: A bullish cross is favorable; a bearish cross is caution.

label: 50/200-day cross
definition: Medium-term vs long-term trend relationship.
how_computed: State of SMA(50) vs SMA(200): golden/death cross, or above/below.
good_when: Golden cross / 50 above 200 = uptrend; death cross / below = downtrend.

label: Trend
definition: Overall direction of the price.
how_computed: Price vs the 50-day, and the 50-day vs the 200-day.
good_when: Uptrend (price > 50d > 200d) is favorable.

label: vs 20-day avg
definition: How far price sits above/below its short-term average.
good_when: Far above (≥~12%) = extended/overbought zone — wait for a pullback, don't chase.

label: vs 50-day avg
definition: How far price sits above/below its medium-term average.
good_when: Modestly above in an uptrend is healthy; far above = extended (don't chase).

label: vs 200-day avg
definition: Price vs its long-term average.
good_when: Above the 200-day is a long-term uptrend.

label: From 52-week high
definition: How far below the past year's high.
good_when: Near 0% = relative strength.

label: Distance to support
definition: How far above the nearest support level.
how_computed: % above the nearest swing-low pivot below price.
good_when: Near support = tightly definable risk and a better add zone.

label: Distance to resistance
definition: How far below the nearest resistance level.
how_computed: % below the nearest swing-high pivot above price.
good_when: A confirmed break above resistance signals momentum.

label: Relative volume
definition: Today's volume vs its recent average — conviction behind the move.
good_when: ≥1.5× confirms a breakout/breakdown; light volume = less conviction.

label: Daily swing (ATR%)
definition: How much the stock typically moves in a day.
good_when: Context: a 4% move is normal for a high-ATR name, alarming for a low one.

label: Breakout
definition: Whether price just made a new short-term high.
good_when: A volume-confirmed breakout is a momentum signal.

label: 1-day return
definition: Price change today.
good_when: Context, not a signal alone.

label: 1-week return
definition: Move over 5 trading days.
good_when: Sustained strength is favorable.

label: 1-month return
definition: Move over ~20 trading days.
good_when: Sustained strength is favorable.

label: Rel. strength vs SPY (1wk)
definition: Is it beating the market this week?
good_when: Positive = leading the market (idiosyncratic strength).

label: Rel. strength vs SPY (1mo)
definition: Is it beating the market this month?
good_when: Positive = leading the market. (Deterioration is judged by the RS trend regime below, not by a single month's wiggle.)

label: RS trend vs SPY (regime)
definition: Is the stock in a persistently outperforming or underperforming regime vs the market — not just a slow week?
how_computed: Mansfield/Weinstein relative strength: the RS line (price ÷ SPY) compared with its own 50-session average. Below the average = underperforming.
good_when: Outperforming. An underperforming regime is a deterioration signal (the standard Stage Analysis sell-side read); needs 50+ sessions of history.

label: Revenue growth (YoY)
definition: Is the top line still growing?
how_computed: (latest-quarter revenue / same quarter a year ago − 1) × 100.
good_when: Stable or accelerating; 15%+ for growth names. Deceleration is a warning.

label: EPS growth (YoY)
definition: Is profit per share growing?
good_when: Stable/accelerating, roughly tracking revenue. Deterioration is a warning.

label: Gross margin
definition: Profitability of each sale.
good_when: Stable or expanding. Falling margins can signal a broken thesis.

label: P/E vs own history
definition: Is it expensive relative to its own past?
how_computed: Current P/E (price ÷ trailing-4Q EPS) vs its ~8-quarter range.
good_when: At/below its own history is fine if growth is intact; stretched + slowing growth is caution.

label: Since entry
definition: Your return since you bought it.
good_when: Informational — not a buy/sell trigger on its own.

label: Weight of book
definition: This position as a % of THIS watchlist sleeve.
how_computed: position market value ÷ total book value.
good_when: INFORMATIONAL ONLY. Never a reason to trim — this is a small satellite sleeve.

label: Distance to stop
definition: How far price is above your stop level (if set).
good_when: A breached stop is a prompt to re-check the thesis — not an auto-cut.

label: Distance to target
definition: How far below your price target (if set).
good_when: Hitting a target is a prompt to reassess, not a forced sell.

label: Reward : risk
definition: Upside to target vs downside to stop.
good_when: ≥ 2:1 is a healthier setup. Informational.

label: Revenue rolling over
definition: Sequential revenue decline — a deterioration signal.
how_computed: Latest-quarter revenue falls vs the prior quarter by ≥ threshold.
good_when: False is good. True contributes to a trim/exit case.

label: Margin compression
definition: Gross margin genuinely deteriorating — not a seasonal mix swing.
how_computed: A mild sequential drop (≥2pp) flags only when the margin is ALSO down vs the same quarter last year; a severe collapse (≥5pp) flags unconditionally. Mild dips are suppressed for hypergrowth names (capacity ramp/mix noise).
good_when: False is good. True contributes to a trim/exit case.

label: Repeated EPS misses
definition: Consistently missing estimates — execution concern.
how_computed: ≥2 of the last 3 quarters reported EPS below estimate.
good_when: False is good. True is a strong deterioration signal.

label: Days to earnings
definition: Time until the next report — the biggest scheduled catalyst.
how_computed: Calendar days to the next confirmed earnings date.
good_when: ≤7 = watch closely; ≤1 = don't size into the print.

label: Analyst consensus
definition: Wall Street's buy/hold/sell tilt.
good_when: A strong buy tilt is supportive; a bearish tilt is caution.
